general_advisory: Regular monitoring for incidence of insect pest should be done as high temperature favors to multiply insect population. Make arrangement for drip irrigation with organic mulch to reduce evaporation loss in standing vegetables and fruit orchard. Considering the forecast of high day temperature in few places, farmers are advised to provide frequent irrigation to the standing crops during evening or early morning hours. Farmers are advised to undertake soil test to know the soil fertility status of their crop field prior to land preparation for Kharif crops. In view of weather forecast farmers are advised to keep animals and birds indoors from 10 AM to 4 PM with provision of sufficient clean drinking water.

advisory: Farmers are advised to arrange certified seeds of paddy from reliable sources like Research institutes, KVK, OUAT, Block Agriculture Office etc.
Seed rate - 20kg/ac for transplanted paddy, 30 -40 kg/ac for manual direct seeding and 10 kg/ac for mechanical direct seeding.
Sowing should be completed by end of June to get higher yield. Arrange good quality seeds from reliable sources like AICRP on Cotton, OUAT and certified dealer etc.
Apply FYM or Compost @ 2 tonnes/acre or Vermicompost @1 tonne/acre uniformly over the soil before ploughing.
Arrange good quality seeds from reliable sources like Research institutes, KVK, OUAT, Block Agriculture Office etc. Sowing should be completed by the first fortnight of June to get higher yield.
In termite endemic area application of Chloropyriphos dust 1.0% @ 10 Kg/ ac at the time of land preparation
Arrange good quality seeds from reliable sources like Research institutes, KVK, OUAT, Block Agriculture Office etc. Sowing can be done from June to first fortnight of July.
In termite endemic area application of Chloropyriphos dust 1.0% @ 10 Kg/ ac at the time of land preparation
Provide shade & plenty of clean drinking water.
Improve ventilation in the barn/shed. Don’t allow them to graze or work between 10 AM to 4 PM.
Don’t allow them to graze between 10 AM to 4 PM.
Allow them to drink water nearby pond during grazing hours.
Don’t over crowd the poultry shed. Provide fan and sprinkle water 2 -3 times per day if required.
